container.title: Rules
container.description:
In choosing units you must ensure you complete no more than 60 credit points at level 1, and at least 36 credit points at level 3 of which at least 24 credit points must be Arts units. It is recommended that you keep your options open in your first year by taking a sequence of units in at least two different areas of study that offer majors within arts.

You may use your Arts electives in Part B. and C. to complete a double major, but please note that no more than two units can normally be credited towards two majors.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure: 2022.19.034

container.title: Part A. Arts listed majors and extended major
APAC  - Psychology extended  major
container.description: If you are completing the APAC Psychology extended major below you will be required to complete 60 credit points of Psychology units and 36 credit points of Arts units which must include Arts electives (12 credit points) and Professional Futures domain units (24 credit points).

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: APAC  - Psychology extended  major
Curriculum Structure Container: Part A. Arts listed majors and extended major

container.title: European languages extended major
container.description: You have the option of reaching proficient level in two European Languages by completing an extended major. You must begin at entry level 2 intermediate level in at least one of the two languages.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure Container: European languages extended major
Curriculum Structure Container: Part A. Arts listed majors and extended major

container.description:
You must complete one of the following majors with no more than 12 credit points at level 1 and at least 18 credit points at level 3. It is recommended that you complete level 2 units in more than one area of arts to give options for finalising your choice of major at level 3.

If you are planning to take the Economics major, please note that background knowledge and application of maths is required at the equivalent of VCE Year 12 Higher level. You may have satisfied this by completing relevant prerequisite unit/s, or by studying relevant topics in your final years of secondary or by successfully completing either ECC1550 Introduction to quantitative skills for social sciences or MTH1010 Functions and their applications, as a first-year elective.

Curriculum Structure Container: Part B. Arts specified study

container.description:
You must complete 24 credit points of Arts elective units and 24 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains.

Note: If you are enrolled in the double degree D3002 Bachelor of Education (Honours) in Secondary Education and Bachelor of Arts you must complete only 12 credit points of units from the Professional Futures domains instead of 24 credit points; and the remaining 12 credit points should be selected from the 2nd year Arts' elective units.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure: 2022.19.034

container.title: Double degree students
container.description: If you are completing a double degree you must complete no more than 36 credit points at level 1 Arts units and at least 36 credit points at level 3 of which at least 24 credit points must be arts units.

parent_record.value: Curriculum Structure: 2022.19.034

container.title: Part C. Free elective study
container.description:
You must complete 48 credit points of free electives from across the university, provided you satisfy the unit rules. Elective units may be chosen from units available in the Bachelor of Arts course to complete a second major or minor(s) from the course. At most, two level one units may be counted towards two majors or a major and a minor.

You are encouraged to take an internship as an elective unit for an opportunity to apply coursework to real world situations and develop workplace skills. In double degree courses, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards the Arts degree.

You can enrich your degree by using some of your free electives to take a study tour, field trip or exchange opportunity. You can also use this space to hone your academic and professional skills with an internship or a range of practical units. See Arts enrichment and Monash Intercultural Lab list of units to discover the breadth of options available.

If you are in a double degree course, some units required for the other degree are credited as electives towards the arts degree.
